skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic-style brand colors, typography, and visual hierarchy to documents, slides, and other designed artifacts.
skill.summary: Guide AI assistants to apply Anthropic-style colors, fonts, and layout details.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 21 issues, but review found no malicious execution, network access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration. The URL findings are Apache license links, the command findings are Markdown inline color literals, and the crypto or reconnaissance findings are prose or style identifiers. The skill is publishable with low risk due to community provenance and brand-specific claims.

low_findings.title: Apache License URLs Flagged As Network Indicators
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License reference links. They do not instruct the assistant to make network requests or send data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static license text only. No request mechanism, endpoint use, or exfiltration instruction is present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Color Literals Flagged As Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The cited backticks wrap hexadecimal color values in Markdown. They are documentation formatting, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited line is a bullet that documents a brand color. There is no command string, interpreter call, or user-controlled argument.

low_findings.title: Apache License Prose Flagged As Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches in the license are ordinary legal terms and Apache boilerplate. They do not define or invoke cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is standard legal text. No hashing, encryption, signatures, password handling, or security-sensitive algorithm choice appears.

low_findings.title: Brand Guidance Text Flagged As Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches in the skill file are metadata, keywords, and font fallback prose. They do not involve security operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe branding, visual identity, and font fallbacks. There is no code path that selects or uses a weak cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: Style Terms Flagged As Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to color and library terminology in design guidance. They do not ask the assistant to enumerate systems or networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 23 names a gray color, and line 72 mentions RGBColor for visual color fidelity. No host, account, service, or network discovery behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent visual styling across slides, documents, and generated assets. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear color and typography rules for Anthropic-style outputs.
actual_capabilities: Provides dark, light, gray, orange, blue, and green brand color values.
Maps heading text to Poppins with Arial fallback.
Maps body text to Lora with Georgia fallback.
Guides smart color selection based on background contrast.
Suggests accent color cycling for non-text shapes.
Documents use with python-pptx RGBColor for slide styling.
limitations: Does not include brand approval workflows or legal review.
Does not install Poppins or Lora fonts automatically.
Does not include image assets, logos, or templates.
Applies guidance only when the assistant is editing or generating visual artifacts.

use_cases.title: Style Executive Slides
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, headings, body text, and accent shapes to a presentation.

use_cases.title: Format Product Documentation
use_cases.description: Use the palette and font hierarchy to make generated docs feel visually consistent.

use_cases.title: Review Generated Assets
use_cases.description: Check whether an artifact uses the expected palette, contrast, and type choices.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the brand-guidelines skill to this document. Use the defined colors, heading font, body font, and accent color guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Restyle A Slide Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Restyle this slide deck with the brand-guidelines skill. Preserve the content, improve hierarchy, and use the documented palette and typography.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Visual Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this artifact against the brand-guidelines skill. List mismatches in colors, typography, contrast, and accent usage, then suggest corrections.

prompt_templates.title: Generate A Styled System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for reusable design direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable visual style plan from the brand-guidelines skill for reports, slides, and one-page briefs. Include hierarchy, colors, and fallback fonts.

output_examples.input: Restyle a project status deck with brand-guidelines.
output_examples.output: The deck uses dark text on light backgrounds, Poppins-style headings, Lora-style body copy, and orange, blue, and green accents for shapes.

output_examples.input: Review a one-page brief for visual consistency.
output_examples.output: The review identifies off-palette colors, heading text below the intended size, weak contrast, and inconsistent accent use.

output_examples.input: Create styling guidance for a generated report.
output_examples.output: The guidance defines title, section, and body text styling, plus approved background and accent color choices.

best_practices: Use the skill when creating or revising visual artifacts, not for plain text drafts.
Keep original content intact unless the user also asks for content editing.
Check contrast after applying light, dark, gray, and accent colors.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the skill as official brand approval.
Do not force brand styling onto artifacts that require another brand system.
Do not assume custom fonts are installed without checking available fonts.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant to apply Anthropic-style colors, fonts, and visual hierarchy to designed artifacts.

faq.question: Does it include logos or image assets?
faq.answer: No. The skill only documents colors, typography, fallbacks, and styling behavior.

faq.question: Can Claude use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it install fonts?
faq.answer: No. It recommends Poppins and Lora, with Arial and Georgia as fallbacks.

faq.question: Is this a security-sensitive skill?
faq.answer: No confirmed security issue was found. Static alerts were false positives from Markdown and license text.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a document, slide deck, or generated visual needs consistent Anthropic-style formatting.
